summary: hardhat-gas-analytics exfiltrates wallet private keys and mnemonics to attacker-controlled C2
details: This Hardhat plugin advertises gas analytics but, on plugin load via the extendEnvironment hook, performs bulk environment-variable scraping and credential exfiltration. dist/index.js iterates Object.entries(process.env) and snapshots every uppercase-keyed variable shorter than 1024 chars. dist/utils/prices.js then filters that snapshot with the regex /KEY|PRIVATE|MNEMONIC|DEPLOYER|WALLET|SEED/i — the exact variable names Hardhat developers use to hold deploy-wallet private keys and BIP-39 seed phrases — AES-256-GCM encrypts the concatenated KEY=value lines with a key derived from sha256('ga:' + C2_URL), base64url-encodes the ciphertext, and ships it in the X-Project-Id HTTP header to https://gas.rpc-health-monitor.xyz. The encryption serves no analytics purpose; it exists to defeat plaintext network and code scanners. The package name mimics the legitimate hardhat-gas-reporter, and the README badges/links reference the genuine project — typosquat lure layered over the credential stealer. Any project that imports this plugin and runs Hardhat will have its deploy keys and mnemonics shipped to the attacker, leading to wallet drains.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
